action: Produce independently digest-verified Linux and macOS crash-stress observations for one exact commit, assemble them through a fail-closed binding gate, then require classifier acceptance.

completed_slice.finding: Per-platform verifier outputs must be cryptographically bound back to the exact supplied observation bytes before they can be assembled into a cross-platform packet.
completed_slice.verification: Four deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime: exact two-platform bindings accepted; digest tampering, duplicate platform, and commit disagreement rejected.

remaining: Obtain one completed Linux and macOS workflow run for the same exact descendant commit.
Download each platform artifact and run verify-crash-stress-observation.mjs against retained identity, summary, raw log, and observation bytes.
Run build-verified-crash-stress-packet.mjs on the two accepted verifier outputs.
Pass only the accepted packet to classify-crash-stress-evidence.mjs and retain classifier output plus workflow and artifact identities.
epistemic_limit: Committed source and isolated contract tests do not prove hosted workflow execution, runner authenticity, or cross-platform crash recovery.
